programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Sports mockumentary about GB Paralympian Darren Silverstone, who stole the hearts of a nation before his infamous chokey-o in Tokyo. For the first time, he tells his story - and starts his glorious comeback. In Tokyo 2020, Darren Silverstone was the Paras GB poster boy. (He was on yoghurts, too.) He cruised to the final and charmed the nation along the way with his signature dance move, the dazzle. But as he took that final bend ready to take gold, Silverstone lost his head and celebrated too soon - and in the process, became a national figure of fun. His subsequent journey in the public eye brought approval and consternation in equal measure: from those unforgettably self-deprecating betting adverts to his turn on The Masked Singer as Breadbin. Darren’s best friend and pilot rider in Tokyo, little Paul Potter, was never seen again in the public eye. The two haven’t spoken since. And now, Silverstone’s back. Ready to take to the tandem at Paris 2024. But is he ready? Who will he be riding with? This is I Am Darren.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and the crew are back in Australia for one of Steve's most dramatic encounters ever as he dangles beneath a helicopter and is dropped into the nest of giant, wild saltwater crocodiles. He also goes in search of the most venomous animal on earth, the box jellyfish - one sting could be fatal, so caution is an absolute must. Finally, he enters a snake-infested marshland on a hunt for two lethal contenders for his list - the tiger snake and the copperhead snake.

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: Vengeance Most Fowl
programme.desc: Wallace invents a 'smart gnome' that has a mind of its own - or does it? It falls to Gromit to take on the dastardly Feathers McGraw once more... or Wallace may never invent again!

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Commissioner Selwyn Patterson struggles in the wake of his shock shooting. As the team rally round him, will he be able to bury his demons and move on? Elsewhere, the local community is rocked by a murder at a care home when a game of bingo gets horribly out of hand. But could someone really kill over something as trivial as that? As the case takes on a series of jaw-dropping twists and turns, Neville and the team realise that this case is so much more than it first appeared on the surface.

programme.title: The Hunger Games: The Ballad of Songbirds & Snakes
programme.desc: Years before he becomes the tyrannical president of Panem, 18-year-old Coriolanus Snow remains the last hope for his fading lineage. With the tenth annual Hunger Games fast approaching, the young Snow becomes alarmed when he is assigned to mentor Lucy Gray Baird from District 12. Putting together their instincts for showmanship and political savvy, they race against time to ultimately reveal who is a songbird and who is a snake.

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: It’s the day of the hustings, and Femi prepares to take on Cheddar. With a lot riding on his success, Femi recruits a reluctant Toby to help him. But Toby is dealing with his own crisis. Omar has a daring plan, while Leah is pulled away from Benjamin by a struggling Felix. Can she find a way to get everything she wants?

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Lord Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her special guests take a deep dive into the candidates' performances and analyse the task, the boardrooms and the firings. Plus, an exclusive chat with the next person to leave the process. This week's guests are Claudine Collins and Owain Wyn Evans.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock are finally back together after Jock makes a lucky escape from his nightmare Colombian prison and becomes a stowaway on a ship bound for Cork.

programme.title: Harry Clark Goes to Rome
programme.desc: From altar boy to Britain’s most famous liar, Harry Clark explores what it means to be a ‘good Catholic’ as he embarks on a pilgrimage to Rome and attempts to meet the new pope.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Can a sweet treat ever be good for you? Zoe Ball is in Birmingham, trying to tempt fitness fanatic Bilal away from sugary sweets and towards flavanol-rich dark chocolate. Chocolate may get a bad press, but the science shows that if you go over to the dark side and eat chocolate with at least 70 per cent cocoa, it could boost your heart health, improve your brain function and even reduce the risk of type 2 diabetes. A delicious upgrade with serious benefits.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Carol Smillie is in for a shock when she sees the state of the property Martin shows her around in Scotland. We also time-travel back to 2006, when Martin saw a house in a stunning location in Wiltshire. Jacqui is in Warwickshire, where outside space is at a premium.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for London and south east England with blockbuster canapes, starters and fish dishes celebrating the British film industry. The dishes are judged by this week's veteran judge, Michelin-starred chef and former banquet fish course winner Aktar Islam, and include a Paddington-inspired take on savoury marmalade and a stuffed poached brill with a Vesper martini broth in a nod to Casino Royale. Who will impress, and who will be sent home?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive loves Venice, but on this trip, he wants to learn what it’s like to be a local, outnumbered by tourists by over 200 to one. He hitches a lift with a rare female gondolier and learns the tough craft of steering with one oar, gets a lesson in glass blowing from his new friend Count Gibi, and finds out why Italian vegetables taste so great as he picks peppers on a farm in the Venetian lagoon.

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: This is the story of two tiny animals coming of age. In the wild woods of North America, a young chipmunk is gathering a vital store of nuts ahead of his first winter - in his way are ruthless rivals and giant predators. In the steaming rainforest, a young tree shrew is forced deep into the jungle to find food. She must draw on all her intelligence and agility if she is to escape the ultimate jungle predator - a reticulated python!

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public. This episode comes from Buxton and features a pair of pistols, a portrait of a girl with whom all men seem to fall in love, a yellow diamond worn for gardening but which could be worth £10,000 and a valuable Steiff bear whose sprightly owner tells Bunny Campione that she was given it for her second birthday - in 1909.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team visit Ashbourne in Derbyshire. Presenter Paul Martin is joined by a team of antiques experts, led by Will Axon and Michael Baggott. Michael spots one of the largest pieces of Moorcroft he has ever seen, and Paul takes time out to enjoy some dry fly-fishing on the River Wye.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan Keating continues his epic journey along Ireland’s Wild Atlantic coast. He makes an emotional trip across the waves with his nephew Conall to Clare Island, a special place for the entire Keating family. They get to grips with a traditional loom, before Ronan heads for Sligo to meet up with a fellow boyband veteran, Shane Filan of Westlife, for some horse riding on the golden sands of Strandhill Beach.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from London and south east England are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating the British film industry and films with links to their region. The dishes are judged by veteran judge Aktar Islam. They include a 28-day dry-aged duck inspired by 28 Days Later, and a peanut and salted caramel parfait that serves as a tribute to the Baftas. But who will be leaving the competition?

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores some of this year's most exciting archaeological finds from the west of Britain. Each discovery comes straight from the trenches/site, filmed by the archaeologists themselves. We discover the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain, and find groundbreaking new evidence that the world-famous Avebury stone circle isn't just a sacred site but a place where our ancestors lived and worked - a discovery that's also changing our understanding of neighbouring Stonehenge. In Staffordshire, the oldest Iron Age gold in Britain is unearthed - a set of beautiful gold torcs, mysteriously abandoned 2,500 years ago.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: With unique access to friends and family including Jackson’s sister La Toya, this episode charts Michael Jackson’s journey from child performer to a deeply troubled musical icon. Behind The Jackson 5, the family act that launched Michael to fame, was a household described by insiders as driven by fear as much as by talent. An offer of a film role helps Michael gain independence from his controlling father, causing tensions and resentments within the family. This leads to Michael's search for authentic connections elsewhere, including among the Jehovah's Witnesses. In 1982, Michael releases his masterpiece Thriller, catapulting him to megastardom and turning him into a global business empire. But the pressures of fame begin to weigh heavily, and colleagues also describe a pattern in Michael’s behaviour: a preference for the company of children.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Ian Fletcher starts a new chapter in Miami as director of integrity for the Fifa World Cup, overseeing the biggest football event in the world. His first challenge is to lead the oversight team towards a decision on which cities should host the semi-final matches. It’s hard enough to get the team to agree, and the fact that it’s also Miami Day on the day of the announcement, and that no-one knows what that is, only serves to complicate things. Meanwhile, the social media team work on a new sustainability initiative based around human waste. The 'power of poop' may just be the solution everyone needs.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Captive Audience: A Real American Horror Story
programme.desc: Two decades after Steven went missing near Yosemite, three tourists vanish in the same area. When the FBI finds the killer, the Stayner family are linked to the new, horrifying crimes.

programme.title: Killing Sherlock: Lucy Worsley on the Case of Conan Doyle
programme.desc: Lucy investigates the return of Sherlock and finds that the darkness of the later stories mirrored the reality of Doyle’s life. After the loss of his eldest son, he became an evangelist for spiritualism. His star declined after a public spat with the magician Houdini. Sherlock Holmes, in contrast, found a life beyond his author on stage and screen.

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: Police begin to piece together the clues after a mother vanishes from her home. What began as a missing person report quickly escalated into one of Scotland’s most high-profile investigations.

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: This episode explores the long legal fight to secure justice for Arlene as the police investigation is rigorously tested through two murder trials and a dramatic appeals process. This journey spans from the High Court in Edinburgh in 2003 to the Scottish Parliament in 2025, where Arlene’s sister Carol continues her determined campaign to uncover the truth about what happened to her sister. Featuring remarkable new testimony from individuals who have never spoken publicly before, this episode follows the enduring commitment of the police and Arlene’s family as they pursue justice and answers.

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Esther are just as surprised as owners Clive and June when they arrive at the site of a burglary to discover the crime scene has been meticulously cleaned and returned to its prior state, despite reports that the room had been turned upside down. Clive’s office is then ransacked before their house is broken into once again, leaving the couple at their wits' end. Meanwhile, despite putting their fostering journey on pause, Humphrey and Martha find their decision back at the forefront of their minds as they await a message from someone from their past. Kelby grapples with Margo’s insistence he keep up positive affirmations whilst dealing with a young teen held captive in a garden shed, and Esther wrestles with a tension headache from a cricked neck and stress. Zoe organises her a massage with a hot new masseuse in Shipton Abbott, but Esther struggles to relieve any tension when she discovers a connection between them.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Seaside serenity beckons in Dorset as Sonali Shah helps Norman and Linda swap city life in London for the calm of the coast. The fantastic line-up of properties includes a mystery house that die-hard fans of the show might just recognise. Sonali also takes a break from house hunting to unwind at a rejuvenating seaweed spa.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp indulges her passion for arts and crafts jewellery with a stunning collection by female designers. Auctioneer Hettie Jago gives Natasha some tips on how to pick up period pieces in the saleroom. Meanwhile, two teams hope their bargains will shine at auction after they shop against the clock at a lively antiques fair at Uttoxeter Racecourse in Staffordshire. In red are mother and daughter Emma and Kate. Emma has the antiques knowledge, and Kate loves to shop – what could possibly go wrong? Over with the blues, three’s a crowd as expectant mum Lucy goes shopping with dad Andrew. He quickly spots an auctioneer’s gavel, but expert John Cameron tells him to hold out for a golden one. Elsewhere, expert Irita Marriott is on the hunt for boxes as the blues hope to put a lid on their challenge. Ruling the rostrum in Lincoln is auctioneer Kirsty Pearson.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With a long Easter weekend to look forward to, Monty is on hand with wheelbarrows of practical gardening advice and inspiration. For people who fancy growing their own potatoes, it's time to get them in the ground. He’ll also be sowing seeds for a colourful cut flower display and starting a new project to show how easy and inexpensive it is to create topiary from yew cuttings. Ashley Edwards heads to a magical spring garden in Horsham to share his love of the season of renewal. In Carmarthenshire, there’s a grower who gave up her job in London to follow her dream of becoming a flower farmer. Plus a gardener in Ely who is simply potty about pots.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: The UK drinks a third of Italy’s Prosecco, but British visitors rarely make it to this corner of the Veneto region. After spending a night in a giant wine barrel, Clive learns why Prosecco is just as good as champagne from one of the country’s few female winegrowers, before jumping into the navigator’s seat in a 70-year-old Maserati and hunting for antiques in the hilltop town of Asolo.

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: This is the ultimate hidden kingdom - the urban jungle. In the colourful and chaotic streets of Rio, a young marmoset is separated from his street gang and forced to confront of the dangers of the city alone. In the futuristic metropolis of Tokyo, a rhinoceros beetle escapes his captors and begins an extraordinary journey through this alien world to find sanctuary.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel invites members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. The team uncover treasures in Kettering, including a selection of gruesome surgical instruments, an assortment of sweetheart badges, fine Worcester porcelain, Frank Ifield's guitar, two paintings by local artist Sir Alfred East, and a pair of surprisingly valuable bedknobs.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It comes from Dorchester, the county town of Dorset. Paul Martin, along with experts David Fletcher and Mark Stacey, uncovers a variety of interesting items, including a 1938 autographed cricket bat and an unusual Clarice Cliff plate. Paul takes a tour around Bovington Tank Museum, where he explores the evolution of the tank from the First World War right up until the present day.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan travels north for the next stop of his Wild Atlantic journey. He grabs his leathers and his helmet, and jumps on his motorbike to explore the north west coast with his great friend, the artist Terry Bradley. He also does some fossil hunting to find the materials to make some extraordinary jewellery, and he doesn’t miss a chance to get on stage to duet with singer Claudia Buckley in one of Donegal’s buzzing country music venues.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from London and south east England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges: Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian and all-round food enthusiast Phil Wang. The guest judge is Simon Callow, the much-loved actor who has starred in such box office hits as Shakespeare in Love, A Room with a View and Four Weddings and a Funeral. How will he judge dishes celebrating the British film industry? Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent London and south east England at the national finals.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Adam and Lucy have a bold idea to save on feed costs, but it means persuading their dairy cows to adopt beef calves and let them suckle. Emma and Rob are taking a holiday before the busy season kicks in, but first they need to get Emma's parents up to speed on running the farm. Ally helps a fellow crofter on an island gather, but low lamb numbers give him cause for concern about when to release his own crop of lambs back out to the hills.

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: HMP Barlinnie is Scotland's largest jail, housing over 1,400 inmates in five gigantic Victorian halls. This series explores the lives of current prisoners and the staff trying to support them as inmates deal with drug-taking, violence and surviving prison life. We also follow prisoners being released back to their families, friends and communities, seeing their challenges and examining the reasons why so many will end up heading back behind bars. In this episode, after overdosing, drug user JP fights for his life in hospital, Norrie threatens suicide while coming to terms with his childhood, and one of Scotland's most violent inmates turns to religion to try and turn his life around.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Angela and her special guests take a deep dive into the candidates’ performances and analyse the task, the boardroom and the firing. Plus, she has an exclusive chat with the next person to leave the process. This week's guests are Ricky Martin and Harriet Rose. Find out more on the website bbc.co.uk/apprentice or by using #TheApprentice

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Katie Razzall.

programme.title: Respect
programme.desc: A biographical drama about the life and rise to fame of American soul singer Aretha Franklin.

programme.title: Kegworth: Flight to Disaster
programme.desc: The air disaster that changed flight safety. Survivors and eyewitnesses tell their story of the terrifying moment Flight BD092 crashed into the side of the M1 motorway.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from London and south east England are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating the British film industry and films with links to their region. The dishes are judged by veteran judge Aktar Islam. They include a 28-day dry-aged duck inspired by 28 Days Later, and a peanut and salted caramel parfait that serves as a tribute to the Baftas. But who will be leaving the competition?

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Under pressure from her mother, Mary weighs up an unconventional offer from Mr Ryder. As Mary grapples with what she truly wants, Lizzy and Mr Collins help Mary chart her own course. And just as Mary thinks she’s taken her future into her own hands, a surprise visit from her aunt and uncle leaves everyone reeling.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Cardiff, Clive Myrie meets business owner Shalize, who wants to find a way to future-proof her health due to a family history of type 2 diabetes. Clive's suggestion is simple, powerful and time-efficient: press-ups! Do them regularly, and the science shows they could reduce the risk of diabetes, lower blood pressure and even help you live longer. A tiny habit with life-changing potential.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After a few decades in Australia, today’s house-hunting couple now want to swap surf and sunshine down under for a new adventure in Cornwall. Jules Hudson helps them on their quest for a home with views and Cornish charm. He also visits an abandoned slate quarry that has been transformed into an extraordinary off-grid retreat, full of creativity and coastal magic.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: If you could do just one thing to transform your health, what should it be? Zoe Ball, Roman Kemp and Clive Myrie show how one small change can make a big difference. From dancing yourself happier to eating mackerel for your heart, to soaking in a hot bath to help you sleep – they share the simple and surprising things we could all do to improve our lives. In postcard-perfect Rye in East Sussex, busy mum Sam is on a mission to supercharge her health after shedding the pounds with the help of weight-loss jabs. Clive Myrie has a simple but powerful idea for her next step – eating oily fish. And it turns out that adding this one food to your diet packs a serious health punch. Omega-3-rich heroes like mackerel, salmon and sardines won’t just help keep the weight off – they could sharpen mental focus, calm inflammation and even lower the risk of heart attacks and strokes.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Sheffield, empty nesters Siva and Dhami are feeling stuck in a rut. But Roman Kemp is about to shake things up. He wants to show them how taking up dancing could do far more than just put a spring in their step. Research reveals that adding dance to your weekly routine can boost fitness, increase brain power and lift your mood. A little rhythm might just rewrite Siva and Dhami's whole routine.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a Â£10,000 question.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Clive Myrie’s Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive heads east towards the wild beauty of Jamaica’s longest mountain range. He catches up with his sister, Judith, who has recently moved from the UK to live on the island. Together, they explore a high altitude coffee farm before Clive heads off to meet descendants of the Maroon community that made this area their home. He also learns the secrets of making authentic Jamaican jerk pork before spending a peaceful evening 100 feet above the waves of the Caribbean Sea.

programme.title: Nature's Epic Journeys
programme.desc: A thousand elephants, from magnificent bulls to vulnerable orphans, come together in a crucial annual gathering in northern Kenya, whilst facing deadly threats from both predators and poachers. Liz Bonnin leads a team of scientists and film-makers using cutting-edge satellite-tracking technology to follow individual elephants day by day as they cope with their daily challenges. The team capture surprising new behaviour and witness how elephant society is responding to a world changed by poaching, including the remarkable way young elephants are coping with the loss of their parents.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel invites members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. Nelson, the Duke of Wellington and Mr Punch all make an appearance when the team visit Hayward's Heath in Sussex. In addition, a ventriloquist's dummy emerges from a box after 30 years and a silver-topped walking stick becomes two feet of 'deadly Toledo steel'. Expert Christopher Payne encounters a dilemma over a table which could be worth thousands more if, as he thinks, it was made on the other side of the Atlantic.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special edition of the programme, from all over the UK. Flog It! revisits some of the historic venues from the series, with brand new valuations and unexpected auctions. Presenter Paul Martin visits Rodmarton, an extraordinary arts and crafts house in the Cotswolds.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Michael Ball, Jodie Prenger, Laurence Rickard and Snoochie Shy take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games suitcase, or will they be tempted with the House of Games bee hotel?

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan completes this leg of his journey along Ireland's Wild Atlantic in County Derry/Londonderry. He walks the 400-year-old walls of the city with comedian Diona Doherty, and hits the farm to find out just what it takes to be a competitive sheep herder, before heading for the windswept coastline of Castlerock to meet a skilled craft worker who builds traditional coracle boats by hand.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Garden experts Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein are joined by guest judge and passionate gardener Dame Prue Leith as they visit three stunning gardens. The first contender is a vibrant three-and-a-half-acre Fernhill garden, lovingly filled with more than 4,000 plants, and owned by Tomas and Thomas in Ballynahinch. Number two is a spectacular suburban garden in Jordanstown created by Karen. And finally, a surprising six-acre farmhouse garden near Crumlin, County Antrim, planted over the last 27 years by Will. Diarmuid, Carol and Prue are searching for a garden which the owners have poured their heart and soul into creating. Which will they decide deserves a place in the final?

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Beechgrove Garden is looking ahead to summer days in the garden, with Lizzie Schofield back to work in the sitooterie area she started off last year as a place ideal for sitting out and enjoying the garden. This week, she prunes and plants in the same area to get it looking great for the good days ahead. Brian Cunningham is back to basics with a beginners' guide to starting off a productive veg plot with some spring sowing, and he also looks at what makes a great homemade compost. Lizzie takes on the tomato plants this year at Beechgrove, and she is looking at what varieties she has picked for 2026. She is also the first of the team to start work on her competition garden, which is based on planting that appeals to one of the senses. Brian is in the 6x8 greenhouse, and there is a trip to Dumfries for the latest from Colin Crosbie’s garden. This week's handy hints are all about what you should be doing with your dahlias right now.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Spring is bursting into life as Frances returns to beautiful Damson Farm, where the garden is waking up in spectacular style. She shares important research from the RSPB on how to urgently support garden birds, reveals how to make the most of sowing older seeds, and gets hands-on preparing vibrant container displays ready for the sunny days ahead. At home, Toby is brimming with ingenious ideas to help you get the most from your outdoor space. Meanwhile, Joe travels to the Cornish coast to meet a gardener who proves that even the wildest seaside weather can’t stop a true planting paradise. There's also a visit to a Cheshire gardener whose passion for Erythronium has created a display that’s genuinely out of this world, plus a viewer in Birmingham who has transformed their garden into a heartfelt celebration of daffodils.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: A new generation of big cats is coming of age. First-time lion mum Serami’s tiny cubs surprise the crew and could mark a split in the Xudum pride. It’s time for Mathata and the oldest cubs in the pride to pull their weight. Pobe the cheetah makes a welcome return with her surviving cub Bo, who finally shows the team what he’s made of. Leopard mum Lediba’s female cub is becoming increasingly independent. But her new-found confidence panics her mum and the crew. And a new character bursts onto the scene, bringing even more tension to the delta.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The uproarious Gina Yashere introduces Lost Voice Guy and Ed Gamble to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Nick Watt.

programme.title: Tenet
programme.desc: A hardened CIA agent is recruited into a mind-bending mystery with cause and effect ‘inverted’, so weapons can strike from the future. His suspect is ruthless Russian oligarch Andrei Sator. Globe-trotting espionage drama with a science-fiction edge from multi-award winning Christopher Nolan, director of Inception, Oppenheimer and Interstellar.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When Penfold saves a celebrity from certain doom he becomes a media sensation. DM smells a rat... or at least some evil villainous hamster from another dimension.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: When the heroes quest to stop Balthazar from turning forest folk into household objects, Herbert realises that he can defeat the wizard’s spell by using the magic tricks he has practiced. Blossom is particularly intrigued by Herbert the Peculiar’s tricks, not to mention his stage name, so when Herbert himself is transformed into a table lamp, it’s Blossom the Very Impressive to the rescue!

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Mouse has built a rocket ship, and fame-seeker Cheese and excitable Dog decide they want to be the first ones to land on Mars. It would be way too dangerous to actually send these two into space, so Mouse stages a fake Mars landing with the help of the others. But when our two astronauts capture the 'aliens', everyone ends up stranded far, far away from home. Like, a 15 minute walk at least.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Heat-loving hippies move into Mr Krabs' soup vat!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job in a cinema projection room, Frog manages to accidentally break the projector. Can Frog save the day and entertain the audience himself?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog are working on a train, but when Frog lets it slip that there is a celebrity on board, the rest of the passengers cause chaos as they try to get a picture.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. The agents struggle to catch an odd creature.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin is inadvertently the getaway driver for a bank robbery.

programme.title: The Primrose Railway Children
programme.desc: When their dad mysteriously disappears, the Robinson family find unexpected excitement, danger and a new sense of belonging on a Highlands heritage steam railway.

programme.title: OOglies
programme.desc: The OOglies slip, slide and splat all over the place. This fast-paced madcap, stop-motion animation uncovers the mischief everyday household objects get up to when no-one is looking. Musical Melon joins a grape boy band, Fishing Finger catches more than he can handle, terrifying Count Grape-ula strikes, the toast soldiers ambush an unsuspecting victim, and the thrill-seeking eggs give a cracking performance in the talent show.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When his beloved plant is sold by mistake, Duck must lose his job at the garden centre in order to retrieve it from a top-security greenhouse.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is in charge of a campsite and has to make sure everyone follows the rules. However, Frog turns up and breaks them all - he lets goats loose and has a midnight party in his tent.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings get a magical Mayan disc that transforms them into mutants.

programme.title: What's New Scooby-Doo?
programme.desc: The gang run into a prisoner ghost while attending the Grind Games.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Patrick's lemonade has a secret ingredient!

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Sandy collect Krabby Patty ingredients.

programme.title: Counterfeit Cat
programme.desc: Max uses a furball enhancer and creates a giant furball baby he must look after.

programme.title: Girl Troop vs Aliens
programme.desc: The Brighthawks hide Ginny’s family as Orbit search camp, friendships are tested, and the camp is locked down. Meanwhile, Ruby and Oz discover the spaceship has been tampered with.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: Aoife’s attempts to avoid the school photo end up with an accidental trip to the other side of town. Can she get back to Midvale in time to stop Jim making a big proposal?

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Can Dr Chris and Dr Xand build a human in the lab just from whizz-bang experiments? Mr Grumbles becomes a mind-bending sculpture, and who will be getting a snotting in Ouchology?

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: While exploring a reef, Shellington and Dashi accidentally trigger a feeding frenzy with multiple sharks biting at anything that moves. As Barnacles and Tweak work frantically to plan a rescue, Shellington and Dashi try not to become shark food!

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: When Dad's Lizard Translator malfunctions, Cosima is carried away by a giant beetle! Cosima and Big Lizard realise they don't need to speak the same language to be best friends.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris must find a solution when a flock of hungry seagulls nearly capsize the iceberg. Can Pancake’s rare, smelly seaweed help them get rid of the unwanted guests and save the day?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck becomes a very serious librarian and makes it his mission to maintain the peace and quiet - that is until Frog arrives and has other ideas.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are relieved that the loud, threatening voice they can hear actually belongs to a small slug named Zorgoth, who is armed with a megaphone. However, when Zorgoth uses his Orb of Compression to shrink the heroes down to the size of ants, their relief evaporates. They must now embark on a long and dangerous quest, through their picnic items and beyond, to warn the Council before Zorgoth shrinks the entire forest.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: Aoife learns that Holt holds the key to the Vipers’ very survival. She tries to save their future by organising the school dance, but will one shocking twist ruin everything?

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall dives into the world of the caiman, learns about the plight of the world’s biggest parrot and tries to find his nemesis - the giant river otter.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Doctor Pfooflepfeffer’s first act as head of governors is to turn Bash Street School into a boarding institution. However, the pupils devise a series of daring attempts to escape.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The blossom has gone missing. No, not Blossom the unicorn, the blossom on the trees! The heroes are sent on a quest deep into the heart of the forest, where legend has it you can see a face watching you from the trees. Twinkle doesn’t believe in legends, but when help comes from nowhere as they try and stop the scoundrel that is stealing the blossom, she is forced to reconsider.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When Mouse brings home an old ice cream truck, the kids start their own smoothie business. However, the town's lovable ice cream man, Uncle Bub, isn't happy about the competition.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a conch decorated with Maori symbols that can control whales.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck must pick up leaves from the park, but Frog's arrival makes it seem impossible. That is, until Frog comes up with a clever solution - using springs from Duck’s beloved chair!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog get a job in a restaurant kitchen, Frog accidentally makes the soup incredibly spicy. He wants to serve it to a celebrity chef, but Duck wants to stop him!

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Memories come flooding back when Orla is asked to build a new airship.

programme.title: Andy and the Band
programme.desc: Pushed out of his comfort zone, Blu must tackle his inability to dance in order to put on a show and impress a celebrity judge. The whole school is counting on him!

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: After being assigned a duet, Noah struggles to find the chemistry onstage with Richelle because of his feelings for Jacquie. LaTroy, Henry and Amy try to play a prank on Ozzy, who teaches his teammates a lesson. Amy's mum discovers that Amy has been lying about being on A-Troupe, and Amy's worst fears may come true.

programme.desc: Gabriel and Penelope face-off and go to extreme lengths to dig for dirt, while Ade and Ede learn that it’s risky to trust a crook.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Cute kittens are infiltrating positions of authority all around the world. Are they the feline equivalent of the illuminati, or do they just want to make napping compulsory?

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The Witch, who was trapped in a Merhaven clam shell while pinching a pearl, grants Blossom a single wish to thank her for releasing her and saving her life. Blossom excitedly chooses a mermaid’s tail to improve her swimming. However, when the Merqueen complains that her pearl is missing, and the heroes have to quest to get it back, Blossom soon realises how tricky a mermaid’s tail can become on land.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Cat decides that she wants to become a superhero, and the other kids decide to help her follow her dream. Girl makes her a cool costume and Mouse creates some superhero gadgets, now all Cat has to do is find somebody to save!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Man Ray rents Squidward's house.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog have a job tidying the mayor’s garden, but then Frog builds an automatic lawnmower, which soon causes chaos. Can Duck get everything back on track in time?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is at the museum to give a really boring talk about dinosaurs. When Frog steps in, he somehow manages to hatch a baby dinosaur from an ancient egg.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. The agents celebrate an important milestone in their partnership.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The team think Raven's supervillain father is the coolest.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Great Barrier Reef, the world's largest structure of living organisms at 1,400 miles long, is home to the most diverse population of undersea life on the planet, and it's under attack! In an epic story torn from the headlines, the Octonauts embark on their largest, most complex rescue mission ever - saving the Great Barrier Reef and all the creatures that live there from an invasion of coral-hungry crown-of-thorns starfish. The Octonauts meet a tiny coral polyp looking to find a home on the very same reef where the Octonauts are deploying their new medical base, the Gup-W, and the Gup-P, a specialised first responder emergency vehicle. To save the reef and their new polyp friend, the Octonauts must combine forces with a diverse crew of zapping, splatting and claw-snapping reef creatures to fend off the spiky, coral-munching starfish.

programme.title: Zagazoo
programme.desc: A tale from Quentin Blake's Box of Treasures. Happy couple Bella and George are surprised to receive a strange parcel containing a baby named Zagazoo, who unexpectedly transforms into a series of badly behaved animals, including a vulture, an elephant, a warthog and a hairy monster, mirroring the various tricky stages of childhood and testing their parental skills to the limit as they try to adapt to their challenging offspring. A touching and hilarious story about growing up and the bond of love that keeps a tight family together.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese

programme.desc: Tim and Tia build a time machine to avoid confronting Dad and telling him the truth. (S2 Ep25)[AD,S]

programme.title: Stan & Gran
programme.desc: Stan and Gran find a mysterious old carved piece of wood while they're beachcombing. Is it from the wreck of Porthole Pete or a carving of a mermaid? (S1 Ep19)[S]

programme.title: PAW Patrol
programme.desc: Chickaletta is left driving the train. (S11 Ep28)[S]

programme.title: Milo
programme.desc: Otto is needed elsewhere by royal appointment, so Milo and pals set to work painting Dame Delores's ballroom. (S1 Ep52)[S]

programme.title: Ben and Holly's Little Kingdom
programme.desc: King Thistle is perplexed when his magic bird-spotting book cannot identify a new creature who makes a nest in the castle. (S1 Ep49)[S]

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Hiccups: Peppa thinks it's funny when George gets hiccups from eating his breakfast too quickly, but then the hiccups start spoiling their game. (S1 Ep16)[AD,S]

programme.title: Odo
programme.desc: Odo tries everything he can think of to help Doodle get over her fear of flying. However, he learns that when a friend has a problem, sometimes the best thing you can do is listen. (S1 Ep12)[S]

programme.title: New: Milkshake! Exploring Senses
programme.desc: Come and explore with David as he discovers all about the sense of taste, and how it helps us understand the world around us. (S1 Ep5)[S]

programme.title: Milkshake! Story Den
programme.desc: Join Derek for a story about the importance of kindness and compassion and all the wonderful ways children can be kind. (S4 Ep25) [S]

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: Start the day with a dose of hard-hitting debate, your phone calls and a little fun - all driven by Jeremy's unique style.[S]

programme.title: Storm & Alexis
programme.desc: Two of 5's much-loved faces team up to focus on the issues that matter most to viewers - with your opinions front and centre.[S]

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: National and international news.[S]

programme.title: Matt Allwright
programme.desc: Matt looks at the big news stories developing during the day and offers help and advice to save you money and make your lives a little easier.[S]

programme.title: Vanessa
programme.desc: Raw, unfiltered, authentic and personal, Vanessa Feltz wants you to tell her what's on your mind. Fiery discussions, a good giggle and plenty of afternoon gossip![S]

programme.title: The Hardacres
programme.desc: Mary and Ma attend their first charitable outing with the society ladies, but it brings them face to face with their challenging past. Joe's head is turned by Lady Adella. (S1 Ep4/6)[AD,S]

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: On the Costa Del Sol, Londoner Carolyn prepares to trial a new menu at her Caribbean restaurant, and in Benidorm, Wayne has a surprise in store for his husband Des. (S10 Ep4)[S]

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Join Dan Walker and the team for the news that matters to you.[S]

programme.title: New: Love Nature
programme.desc: A look at a species of auk called black guillemots, known locally as Tysties, that nestle in the harbour wall of Oban Bay on the west coast of Argyll, arriving in May to begin nesting. (S1 Ep41)[S]

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Susan goes on a quest in Somerset, hunting for signs of King Arthur, taking in Glastonbury Abbey, possibly the site of his tomb; West Somerset Railway; and the home of Clarks shoes. (S9 Ep6)[S]

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: A woman pulled over for driving without a full licence has her own theories as why she's been stopped. And reports come in of a car that's linked to drugs. Will the roadside drug test confirm suspicions?

programme.title: New: Building the Impossible...
programme.desc: ...with Rob Bell. Rob travels to Boston Harbor to explore America's first lighthouse - the 'cursed beacon' Boston Light - in a story of civil war, tragedy, technology and innovation. (S3 Ep6/6)[S]

programme.title: New: Jane McDonald in Nashville
programme.desc: Jane visits the Bluebird Cafe for open mic night, braves Nashville's hot chicken; tours the Grand Ole Opry; and ends her trip on Broadway with - of course! - a country song performance! (Ep2/2)[AD,S]

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em: Secrets & Scandals
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes of the classic Michael Crawford comedy reveals how the show's iconic scenes were made. Plus, an exclusive interview with show creator Ray Allen.

programme.title: New: Piers Morgan: Uncensored
programme.desc: The bold, opinion-led news and debate show hosted by Piers Morgan. Piers blends journalism, debates and cultural commentary that's unapologetically direct. (S2 Ep12)[S]

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 10
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a one bedroomed house in Yorkshire, a two bedroom flat in Kent and a semi detached house in Birmingham.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view an end-of-terrace in Cornwall, a barn in Cheshire and a flat in Middlesex.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 18
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a bungalow in Derby, a London property that dates back to Victorian times, and a South Wales terrace.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 9
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a three bed Semi in Shropshire, a different sort of property in the Kent countryside and a terrace in Derby with features you won't want to keep.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 34
programme.desc: Lucy and Martin visit a cottage in Grewelthorpe, Yorkshire, a stunning Victorian house with space in Yelverton, Devon and a former library in Middleton, Manchester which looks okay from the outside but you can't judge a book by its cover!

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 22
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a three bed terraced property in Accrington, Lancashire, a property in need of full scale renovation in Kent and a barn in Devon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 1
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a property in Stockport, there's a Maidstone office with planning permission for six flats and finally a Telford house with some easy renovation opportunities

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 18
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a cottage in Devon, a converted terraced property in London and a house in Leeds.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 14
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a property in Preston, a property in Southsea and a terraced house near Durham with a surprise.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 21
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a confined ground floor flat in Wiltshire, a cottage in Kent and a plot of land in Yorkshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 3
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a grand farm house worth getting excited about in Cheshire, in Dorset there's an old wooden chapel praying for help from above and in Stoke there's a spacious semi with no space.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 7
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a three bedroom semi detached house in Nottingham, a Georgian terraced house in London and a workshop in Devon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 28
programme.desc: Lucy and Martin visit a grand block of flats in Herne Bay with views of the sea. They also take a look at a bungalow in Derbyshire with enough space to build a second property and a Georgian property in Preston with four floors in need of a makeover.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: The iconic TV series is back, but in a brand-new setting with brand-new prize boxes and a new batch of contestants hoping to win a whopping cash prize - who will have what it takes to beat our brand-new Banker? Now hosted by Stephen Mulhern, the series will see contestants battle it out against the notorious Banker to be in with a chance of winning a life-changing cash prize in a nail-biting game of nerves and intuition. Contestants are confronted with 22 sealed boxes with each one hiding a different amount of money - without knowing what each one contains, the contestant must eliminate the boxes, which are opened one at a time, and the amount of money inside is revealed. Pressure mounts as the player is tempted by the notorious Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for what might be inside their box, and as long as the larger cash prizes remain unchosen, the Banker's offers will get higher... but that can all change in an instant if a big cash prize is unveiled.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Faith Alexander arrives in Midsomer Deverell to meet her long-lost uncle Rex Masters, but Rex has told everyone that Faith's entire family was wiped out in a plane crash and he has disappeared - thanks to Miles King, an old friend who does Rex's gardening, news spreads quickly as Rex's cook Eileen Carnack, cleaner Janet Bailey, and Janet's fiance Jeremy Thacker rush to his home at Stonecroft House. Faith is anxious and can't understand how Rex has photographs of her when they haven't met following a 40-year family feud - after Barnaby asks Jones to investigate the disappearance, Rex's body is found in a river below Crofter's Weir. As Barnaby returns to Rex's study and finds some important clues in a set of risque photographs from the 1960s, can he get to the bottom of the tangled relationships stretching across the generations, learn the truth about Rex's life of mischief-making, and identify the killer?

As things get slightly out of hand, Gina has to decide whether to accept Charlie's offer of a move to London or not - meanwhile, Greengrass acquires a couple of classic English motorbikes claiming that they are for a regimental museum... but in fact he plans to sell them on to Charlie!

programme.title: Heartbeat

As a coach trip to Whitby organised by Gina becomes a welcome day out for the locals and Kate and Nick are dragged into a desperate situation when a little girl loses her father on the beach, Greengrass is in trouble again... and this time it's for years of tax evasion.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series about a GP who relocates to the sleepy Cornish hamlet of Portwenn. Martin meets the village's new midwife Molly as she will be looking after Louisa, but Martin is dismissive of her profession and the two of them do not hit it off - meanwhile, Bert and Al have a new cook at their restaurant, called Marigold, who surprises them both one morning by bringing in some dead pheasants that she claims to have caught in a snare. When Marigold cuts her finger and Bert protectively rushes her to the surgery, Marigold tells Martin that her sister has glandular fever and Martin orders her to take extra hygiene precautions around the food she cooks - elsewhere, Martin visits Louisa's school to perform hearing assessments on the children when Louisa reveals that she is having 'toilet troubles'. Martin is quick to diagnose an urinary tract infection and writes a prescription for her, but Molly advocates a more natural holistic approach - whose treatment method will win the day?

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series about a GP who relocates to the sleepy Cornish hamlet of Portwenn. Joan starts a small bed and breakfast business to solve her farm's money problems - her first guest is local farmer Ted, who has also got into financial difficulties. They're a good match initially, but Joan soon finds that Ted is far from the perfect guest when he has a suspected mild heart attack at her farm and Martin finds a mysterious lump in his abdomen - elsewhere, Pauline confronts Martin about his contract offer for the London job. When Pauline ends up being ejected from the surgery while trying to demonstrate her worth, an impromptu afternoon by the seaside with Al goes wrong when she is stung by a jellyfish and must seek out Martin for help. Meanwhile, Louisa finds it hard to enjoy her baby shower because everyone wants to talk about Martin leaving Portwenn, and the Doc joins Edith at a hotel where she is giving the keynote speech to a medical conference about her research.

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered how clever you are? Do you think you might be in the top 50 per cent of the country, the top ten per cent, or even the top one per cent? Comedy legend Lee Mack is about to find out as he hosts another edition in the second series of the show. The 1 Club is the ultimate leveller - it doesn't matter if you're not usually good at quizzes, because the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts... they're all about logic and common sense. It doesn't matter if you're 15 or 50 - this is the quiz that the whole family can play together. 100 contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question that only one per cent of the country can get right... and win up to 100,000 pounds?

programme.title: Rehnaa Hai Terre Dil Mein
programme.desc: A romantic drama that explores the emotional journey of two souls intertwined by love, where a man pretends to be someone else to win the heart of the woman he loves.

programme.title: Barsaat (1995)
programme.desc: Badal and Tina's love faces major hurdles when her dad disapproves and unjustly locks Badal up for a crime he didn't commit. Can their love survive this tough situation?

programme.title: Jodi No 1
programme.desc: Jay and Veeru, on the run from a local goon, set their sights on a wealthy businessman. They hatch a daring plan that promises excitement and danger ahead!

programme.title: Hero Hindustani
programme.desc: A London girl, pushed by her grandfather to marry in India, hires a charming guide to act as her fiance. But things get complicated when she unexpectedly falls for him!

programme.title: Pardes
programme.desc: Kishorilal arranges his westernized son's marriage to Ganga, brought from India to the USA, where Ganga forms a deeper connection with Kishorilal's foster son, Arjun.

programme.title: Aakhree Raasta
programme.desc: Falsely imprisoned for his wife's murder, a man escapes to seek revenge. But his mission takes an emotional turn when the determined cop chasing him is revealed to be his estranged son.

programme.title: Badal
programme.desc: Badal loses his family in a violent riot and is raised by a terrorist. His life becomes a mission to hunt down the police officer responsible for the tragedy.

programme.title: Sham Ghanshyam
programme.desc: Two brothers set out to avenge their father's death by targeting a corrupt collector, igniting a gripping story of justice, family loyalty, and the quest for redemption.

programme.title: Police Aur Mujrim
programme.desc: A gangster is out for blood after his henchmen get locked up by the police. Fueled by vengeance, he's determined to make them pay for their betrayal.

programme.title: Awarapan
programme.desc: A hitman is asked to keep an eye on the boss's mistress. He begins to fall for her before he realises she is using him to get her freedom, which forces him to choose between divided loyalties.

programme.title: Daringbaaz

programme.title: Badle Ki Raat Revenge
programme.desc: Starring: Sivaji,Kainaz Motivala. Kartik and Shravani's romantic weekend turns terrifying when their farmhouse reveals creepy secrets. Strange noises and a chilling presence turn love into a spine-tingling haunted experience.

programme.title: Dharti Putra

programme.title: Kisi Ka Bhai Kisi Ki Jaan

programme.title: Geeta Govinda
programme.desc: Starring: Deverakonda, Rashmika Mandanna. An innocent young lecturer is misunderstood as a pervert and despised by a woman who co-incidentally turns out to be the younger sister of his brother-in-law.

programme.title: Jaanwar
programme.desc: Starring: Akshay Kumar,Karisma Kapoor. A reformed criminal, Babu finds purpose in raising a train crash survivor as his own. But when his past resurfaces, his newfound happiness hangs in the balance.

programme.title: Commando 3
programme.desc: Starring: Vidyut Jammwal,Adah Sharma. Officers Karan and Bhavana embark on a high-stakes mission to London, racing against time to stop a terrorist plot threatening their homeland, India.

programme.title: Skanda
programme.desc: Starring: Ram Pothineni,Sreeleela. Renowned for martial arts, a young man is recruited by a secret agency. His mission: confront terrorists, face danger, and fight bravely to safeguard peace and innocent lives.

programme.title: Madhura Raja
programme.desc: Starring: Mammootty,Jagapathi Babu. Raja, a lionhearted man, heads to a village to protect the people who are being terrorised by a local criminal and illegal liquor merchant, Nadeshan.

programme.title: Masss
programme.desc: Starring: Suriya Sivakumar,Nayanthara. After a life threatening accident, a man finds that he can communicate with the dead, and must decide whether to act on their advice.

programme.title: Bhajarangi 2
programme.desc: Starring: Shiva Rajkumar,Shruthi. A forest officer uncovers drug baron Araka's scheme: exploiting villagers for drug production. Driven, the officer resolves to halt the operation.
